July 7, 1923: A balloon race ends in tragedy for the two-man crew of a Navy craft found wrecked in Lake Erie, three days after it launched in Indianapolis. Lt. Louis Roth is found dead apparently from the crash impact; Lt. Telford Null drowned. "All over" is written in their log.

During the 1986 Cleveland Balloonfest over 1.5 million balloons were released simultaneously. The event was intended to be a harmless fundraising publicity stunt, but the balloons drifted back over the city, Lake Erie, and landed in the surrounding area, causing problems for traffic and a nea
In 1986, Cleveland released 1.5 million balloons, but winds turned the stunt into disaster, flooding streets and Lake Erie and causing two deaths.

The Disappearance of the F-89 In November 1953, U.S. Air Force radar detected an unknown object entering U.S. airspace over Lake Superior. A Northrop F-89 Scorpion fighter with Lieutenants Felix Moncla and Robert Wilson on board was dispatched to intercept it.

Two radar blips merged into one over Lake Superior in 1953, then both vanished. The F-89 jet and unknown object disappeared forever. Still unexplained.
